model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Michigan State is the safer pick because the typical 3/14 gap in high-major athleticism, depth, and defensive physicality tends to show up over 40 minutes, especially against a Summit League opponent stepping up in competition. North Dakota State has a credible upset path if it shoots well from three and keeps Michigan State out of transition while avoiding live-ball turnovers, but that’s a narrower, higher-variance route. With no clear, verified availability red flags surfaced in the quick news scan, I’m siding with Michigan State’s higher baseline quality and ability to win even if the shooting is merely average.

reasoningStep.title: East 3/14: Michigan State over North Dakota State
reasoningStep.summary: Back the 3-seed’s two-way floor and depth; NDSU needs a hot-shooting, low-turnover game to spring the upset.
evidence: Local combined KenPom/Torvik lookup returned null for both teams in the available file, so the pick leans on seed-quality priors and matchup dynamics rather than a specific rating edge.
Web/ESPN quick scans did not surface a concrete Michigan State injury/availability issue that would materially change the baseline expectation.

Upset risk is acknowledged via NDSU’s plausible variance-based path (3-point shooting + turnover margin), but Michigan State’s physicality, rebounding, and defensive consistency provide more stable win conditions.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
